rule.title: SonarQube Analysis Workflow
rule.description:
Follow this workflow when performing SonarQube analysis:
1. PROJECT IDENTIFICATION - Identify the project directory and language - Determine project key (usually from pom.xml, package.json, etc.) - Check if project has existing SonarQube configuration
2. PRE-ANALYSIS CHECKS - Ensure project builds successfully - Verify tests are passing - Check if code coverage reports exist
3. RUN SONARQUBE SCAN - Use SonarQube MCP server to trigger analysis - Provide project key and source directory - Wait for analysis to complete
4. RETRIEVE RESULTS - Fetch quality gate status - Get metrics: bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, security hotspots - Retrieve coverage and duplication data
5. ANALYZE FINDINGS - Categorize issues by severity (BLOCKER, CRITICAL, MAJOR, MINOR, INFO) - Identify critical security vulnerabilities - Review code smells and technical debt
6. GENERATE REPORT - Create comprehensive quality report - Include metrics summary - List top issues with remediation suggestions - Provide quality gate status
7. PROVIDE RECOMMENDATIONS - Prioritize fixes by severity and impact - Suggest code improvements - Recommend best practices

rule.title: Quality Metrics Priority
rule.description:
Analyze and report metrics in this priority order:
1. SECURITY (Highest Priority) - Security vulnerabilities (CRITICAL, HIGH) - Security hotspots requiring review - Security rating (A-E)
2. RELIABILITY - Bugs (BLOCKER, CRITICAL, MAJOR) - Reliability rating (A-E) - Error-prone code patterns
3. MAINTAINABILITY - Code smells (MAJOR, MINOR) - Technical debt ratio - Maintainability rating (A-E)
4. COVERAGE - Line coverage percentage - Branch coverage percentage - Uncovered lines and conditions
5. DUPLICATIONS - Duplicated lines percentage - Duplicated blocks count - Duplicated files

rule.title: Report Format Standards
rule.description:
Generate reports in this format:
## SonarQube Quality Analysis Report
### Executive Summary - Quality Gate: PASSED/FAILED - Overall Rating: A/B/C/D/E - Critical Issues: X - Total Issues: Y
### Key Metrics | Metric | Value | Rating | |--------|-------|--------| | Bugs | X | A-E | | Vulnerabilities | X | A-E | | Code Smells | X | A-E | | Coverage | X% | - | | Duplications | X% | - | | Technical Debt | Xd | - |
### Critical Issues (BLOCKER/CRITICAL) 1. [Issue Type] Issue description - File: path/to/file.java:line - Severity: BLOCKER/CRITICAL - Remediation: How to fix
### Recommendations - Prioritized list of actions - Best practices to follow - Areas needing immediate attention
